fixtures.description: Bandlimited white noise 100 Hz - 18 kHz. Near-flat spectrum with no peaks above contour.
fixtures.notes: Negative control. No algorithm should flag anomalies here.

fixtures.description: Bandlimited noise + single +18 dB peaking EQ at 500 Hz (Q=8).
fixtures.notes: True anomaly. Current algo will likely miss it (500 Hz is not within 3 dB of spectral global max for wideband source). New algo should catch it via local-contour comparison.

fixtures.description: Bandlimited noise + three true resonances at 500, 1500, 4000 Hz (+18/+16/+14 dB, Q=8).
fixtures.notes: Three clearly isolated true resonances. New algo should catch all three.

fixtures.description: Percussive hits with three inharmonic tonal modes at 2500, 3800, 6200 Hz. These are the instrument's natural timbre, NOT resonance anomalies.
fixtures.notes: CANONICAL FALSE-POSITIVE CASE. Current algorithm flags the tonal modes because they sit at/near the global max of the narrow spectrum. New algorithm must NOT flag them: in the local-contour view they ARE the contour, not spikes above it. Success criterion: new algo reports 0 anomalies here.

fixtures.description: Same spoon percussion as 04, with an ADDITIONAL +20 dB narrow resonance at 1200 Hz (Q=12) — a real anomaly below the natural content.
fixtures.notes: Tests that new algo distinguishes natural tonal content (2500/3800/6200 Hz modes) from a genuine narrow resonance (1200 Hz) added below them. The 1200 Hz peak is isolated, sharp, and well above its local contour. New algo should flag ONLY 1200 Hz.

fixtures.description: Bandlimited noise 30 Hz - 18 kHz + strong +18 dB peaking EQ at 80 Hz (Q=8).
fixtures.notes: Tests the frequency floor. Old algo uses 100 Hz floor (misses this). New algo uses 60 Hz floor (catches this).

fixtures.description: Bandlimited noise + mild peaking EQ at 800 Hz, only +6 dB (below +9 dB detection threshold).
fixtures.notes: A +6 dB bump is in the 'musical' range, not a problematic resonance. New algo should NOT flag it (below +9 dB threshold).

fixtures.description: Bandlimited noise + three narrow resonances at 800, 900, 1000 Hz (+16 dB each, Q=15).
fixtures.notes: Tests the moving-average window size. If window is too wide (e.g. ±1 octave), the 3 close peaks collectively raise the local average and none of them reads as 'peak above neighborhood' → test fails. With ±1/3 octave window, each should still exceed its own immediate neighborhood by enough to be flagged.

fixtures.description: Realistic mix: kick (pitch-glide 100-50 Hz), bass tone 80 Hz, synth with +18 dB resonance at 2500 Hz, hi-hat bursts.
fixtures.notes: Realistic scenario. Kick fundamental and bass fundamental are NOT anomalies (natural musical content). The 2500 Hz synth resonance IS an anomaly. New algo should flag ONLY 2500 Hz.
